errors_found.description: The original report.json files included an 'insight' score (subjective quality rating, 1-3) in each atom's 'total' and 'max_possible'. The corpus rubric keys do NOT include 'insight' — only boolean/count items. This inflated max_possible from 84 to 143 (adding 59 points of insight max across 20 atoms) and inflated total scores by the sum of insight values.
errors_found.impact: Percentages were artificially compressed toward a higher denominator, making models appear closer together than they actually are on rubric accuracy alone.

errors_found.description: Atom 15's corpus rubric has 5 keys (prereq_count, phase_count, health_checks_count, has_rollback_trigger, has_final_owner) but all three reports scored only 4 keys (missing has_rollback_trigger) and set rubric_max=4 instead of 5. This is a scoring gap — the 'has_rollback_trigger' criterion was never evaluated.
errors_found.impact: All three models lost 1 potential point on atom 15. Corrected rubric_max for atom 15 is 5, not 4.

what_changed.ranking: Original: glm-5.2 (88.8%) > kimi-k2.7 (88.1%) > deepseek-v4 (85.3%). Corrected: glm-5.2 (91.7%) > deepseek-v4 (90.5%) > kimi-k2.7 (89.3%). deepseek-v4-pro-cloud moves from 3rd to 2nd place, overtaking kimi-k2.7-code-cloud.
what_changed.spread: Original spread: 3.5 percentage points. Corrected spread: 2.4 percentage points. Models are closer on pure rubric accuracy.
what_changed.best_model: glm-5.2-cloud remains the best model in both original and corrected scoring.
what_changed.deepseek_improvement: deepseek-v4-pro-cloud's corrected percentage (90.5%) is significantly higher than its original (85.3%) because it had lower insight scores (total insight=43) compared to glm-5.2 (total insight=50) and kimi-k2.7 (total insight=51). Removing insight penalized deepseek less.

corrected_recommendation.explanation: The original policy recommendation favored glm-5.2-cloud as the best model. The corrected scoring confirms glm-5.2-cloud remains the top performer (91.7% vs 90.5% and 89.3%). The ranking order changes slightly (deepseek-v4-pro-cloud moves to 2nd, kimi-k2.7-code-cloud to 3rd) but the top recommendation is unchanged. The corrected data provides a cleaner view of rubric compliance without subjective insight noise.
